checks.name: psutil available (RSS tracking)
checks.detail: install psutil for full memory metrics

B_massive_cold.description: 120 directives, cold — I/O and parse overhead

C_single_shot.description: Single-shot renders (10 runs), one process per render, zero prior state

D_single_dir_warm.description: 1 directive warm — best case, minimal content

E_cache_miss_storm.description: 20 renders, each with 5 unique directive keys — sustained cache miss

F_state_a_baseline.description: Empty context — State A baseline, overhead = 0, token savings = 0

gates.name: No cold renders fully timed out
Warm cache faster than cold (all cells)
gates.note: Some cells may be cold≈warm due to I/O noise on fast disks

gates.name: Warm speedup >=1% for large contexts (>=30 directives, tier 3)
gates.note: @env directives not disk-cached; warm benefit visible with @memory/@query/@include

gates.name: Error rate <=1% at peak concurrency (250)
gates.note: At conc=250 macOS/Linux RLIMIT_NPROC may cause fork failures; run `ulimit -u unlimited` before benchmarking at extreme concurrency

gates.name: Throughput does not collapse at 250 concurrent (>=10% of tps@10)
Tier 1 produces <= tokens than tier 3 (all directive counts)
No overhead-dominant scenarios detected
gates.note: overhead_detected=True means Perseus adds more cost than benefit in specific edge cases

gates.name: Warm 1-directive faster than cold 1-directive
gates.note: @env warm≈cold by design; this gate catches regressions when caching is active

gates.name: Enterprise day cost ROI positive
gates.note: Assumes context re-description cost = 10x Perseus output tokens
